steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage shows clam size and productivity increased even as harvesting pressure rose. The question asks what conclusion this supports about the gardening practices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — contemporary Kwakwaka'wakw people have only minimally deviated from the clam-gardening practices described in the people's songs.: The passage doesn't address present-day use. ✗
  • Choice B — the Kwakwaka'wakw people initially adapted their clam garden practices from similar methods observed among other Indigenous peoples in neighboring areas.: Origin of the practice is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — the practical approach described in the Kwakwaka'wakw songs effectively maintained clams as a sustainable food source.: Larger, more productive clams under greater harvesting demand is direct evidence of sustainability. ✓
  • Choice D — the Kwakwaka'wakw people encouraged the health of clam gardens and reduced pressure on them by periodically increasing their reliance on other sources of sustenance.: Contradicts the passage, which states harvesting pressure increased. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
